data.title: Reddit stock fell on Thursday due to mixed ad feedback, following report by advertising agencies.
data.description: The value of Reddit stock decreased on Thursday due to reports of varied opinions from advertising agencies. An analyst's warning highlighted the mixed feedback, leading to a slump in Reddit's stock. This information was shared on Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Senate delays meeting on Clarity Act after Coinbase opposes parts of crypto market bill.
data.description: Meetings regarding the Clarity Act in the Senate committees have been postponed due to opposition from Coinbase over certain aspects of the crypto market structure bill. The delay comes after Coinbase raised concerns about specific provisions in the bill. This setback has caused a halt in the progress of the Clarity Act discussions. The outcome of the delay has yet to be determined, with the postponement impacting the advancement of the bill in the Senate committees. Stay tuned for further developments on this matter.

data.title: Alibaba stock dipped initially but recovered, following the announcement of a major update to its Qwen AI app for shopping.
data.description: After unveiling a significant update to its Qwen AI app, which is geared towards shopping, Alibaba's stock experienced a decline. The Chinese tech giant witnessed a decrease in its stock price following the announcement. Conversely, the stock had been on an upward trend prior to this development. This adjustment to the Qwen AI app signifies Alibaba's commitment to enhancing its shopping experience through artificial intelligence technology. This move by the company may have impacted investor sentiment, resulting in the stock dip. The specific numbers related to the stock decline and subsequent rally were not provided in the original information.

data.title: RLH Equity announced Keystone's technology unit acquired by Audax Private Equity, boosting global expansion.
data.description: On January 15, 2026, RLH Equity Partners shared the news of the acquisition of the global technology and advisory services division of Keystone, a company in their portfolio, by Audax Private Equity. This decision marks a significant development in the industry and reflects the growing trends in mergers and acquisitions. The specific details and financial terms of the deal were not disclosed publicly. This transaction showcases the ongoing evolution and consolidation within the technology and advisory sectors, underscoring the dynamic nature of the market.

data.title: Polygon Labs acquires Coinme for $250M, entering stablecoin payments market. 6 new stablecoin projects announced globally since Dec 1, 2025.
data.description: Polygon Labs, a blockchain company, is acquiring crypto payments company Coinme and crypto infrastructure provider Sequence for $250 million to enter the stablecoin-based payments market. The founder of Polygon Foundation, Sandeep Nailwal, aims for Polygon to become the largest stablecoin money movement platform globally. Stablecoins, digital tokens pegged to fiat currency, gained traction after the passage of the Genius Act. This year has seen the rise of stablecoins from a crypto trading primitive to a payments primitive, with six new stablecoin projects announced or launched globally since December 1, 2025. Notable projects include SoFiUSD, USD1 in Pakistan, EURXM, USDXM, RONXM by a Romanian bank, and Wyoming FRNT stablecoin. Despite the influx of stablecoins, market dominance remains with Tether and USDC. The necessity of more stablecoins for everyday payments is questioned, with factors like liquidity, compliance, and integration being crucial for merchant and payment service provider preferences. New stablecoins are more welcomed if they're engineered for specific protocols.

data.title: ProboTalent appoints Dr. Nathan Mondragon as President and Chief Strategy Officer, aiming for market dominance and high-growth.
data.description: In New York, ProboTalent, an AI-native talent intelligence platform rooted in psychometric science, has made a significant leadership announcement. They have appointed Dr. Nathan Mondragon as their new President and Chief Strategy Officer. Dr. Mondragon, a well-known figure in Human Capital Management technology, will be responsible for guiding ProboTalent through its upcoming stage of growth and innovation. This move comes as the company gears up for rapid expansion in the market.

data.title: Dow Jones up due to jobless claims; Nvidia supplier TSMC surges. Nasdaq-100, small caps lead gains in stock market.
data.description: Thursday saw a notable increase in the Dow Jones index following a surprising drop in weekly jobless claims. The chipmaker Nvidia experienced a significant surge in the market, with TSMC leading the way due to positive earnings. The Nasdaq-100 and small-cap stocks notably showed gains during the day’s trading session. Additionally, companies like Coherent and Cameco cleared entry points, attracting investor interest. These updates were part of the live coverage of the day's stock market activities.

data.title: Dr. David Brand joins Angeleno Group as Operating Partner, bringing expertise in sustainable forestry and climate solutions.
data.description: A Los Angeles investment firm, Angeleno Group, focusing on clean energy and climate solutions, recently welcomed Dr. David Brand as the new Operating Partner. Dr. Brand, an expert in sustainable forestry and nature-based climate investing, is known for his leadership at New Forests, where he served as Founder, former CEO, and Executive Chair. New Forests is a prominent investment management company with a global presence. This move signifies Angeleno Group's commitment to expanding its expertise in the field.

data.title: LivsMed completes largest 2025 KOSDAQ IPO, raising $94M (₩135.9B) at ₩55,000/share for market cap of ₩1.4T ($1B) to expand globally.
data.description: A South Korean minimally invasive surgical device company, LivsMed, has successfully completed its initial public offering on the KOSDAQ market, raising approximately $94 million (₩135.9 billion). Priced at ₩55,000 per share, the IPO has propelled the company's market capitalization to over ₩1.4 trillion (about $1 billion), solidifying its position as a leading Medtech "Unicorn." The funds raised will be utilized for establishing an integrated production facility and supporting continued research and development efforts.

data.title: ImmunityBio sees a 700% revenue surge in 2025 with preliminary net product revenue of $113 million for Anktiva, alongside a 750% increase in unit sales volume compared to 2024.
data.description: ImmunityBio, Inc. (NASDAQ: IBRX) saw a surge in shares on Thursday due to its impressive execution and sales performance, reporting a substantial rise in preliminary net product revenue. For fiscal 2025, the company announced that their product Anktiva brought in around $113 million in net product revenue, marking a remarkable 700% increase from the previous year. Anktiva, the first FDA-approved immunotherapy for non-muscle invasive bladder cancer carcinoma in situ (CIS), exhibited significant growth. In the fourth quarter of 2025, ImmunityBio reported revenue of approximately $38.3 million, surpassing the previous quarter’s $31.8 million, reflecting a 20% quarter-over-quarter increase and a 431% year-over-year increase. Furthermore, the company noted a 750% surge in unit sales volume for Anktiva in 2025 compared to 2024. This exceptional growth showcases the company’s strong quarter-over-quarter revenue progression.

data.title: State Street (NYSE: STT) introduces digital finance infrastructure, gears up for earnings. Analysts predict $2.84 EPS, $3.62B revenue.
data.description: State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) shares saw a slight increase as the company introduced a new digital finance infrastructure to connect traditional and blockchain ecosystems. The stock is currently challenging resistance and hit a new high. The announcement precedes the firm's earnings release on Friday, January 16. Analysts predict the Boston-based company will post quarterly earnings of $2.84 per share, higher than the $2.60 per share reported in the same period last year. The consensus estimate for State Street's quarterly revenue stands at $3.62 billion. For more information, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: BD wins FDA clearance for new breast biopsy system, launching in early 2026. Features advanced capabilities. Market cap $59.10B, stock 52-week range $162.29-$251.99.
data.description: On Thursday, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration cleared Becton, Dickinson and Company's new breast biopsy system, the EnCor EnCompass Breast Biopsy and Tissue Removal System, under the 510(k) process. The system is set to be launched in early 2026, aiming to improve the biopsy process by enabling clinicians to conduct procedures using various breast imaging methods. It offers advanced features such as dual vacuum strengths, 360-degree sampling capability, and multiple probes for different types of lesions. Becton, Dickinson and Company, trading under NYSE: BDX, holds a market capitalization of $59.10 billion, with its stock price fluctuating between $162.29 and $251.99 in the past 52 weeks. This development stands out amidst a generally positive trend in major stock market indices. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson's drug slows blood cancer, extends survival in pretreated patients in a Phase 3 study for multiple myeloma.
data.description: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) released findings from the Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 study of Tecvayli (teclistamab-cqyv) monotherapy for multiple myeloma, a form of blood cancer. The trial compared Tecvayli against the standard treatments of pomalidomide, bortezomib, and dexamethasone (PVd) or carfilzomib and dexamethasone (Kd) in patients with relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma who had undergone 1 to 3 previous lines of therapy. This study marks the second Phase 3 trial supporting Tecvayli as a potential new standard of care from the first relapse onwards. Significant survival data were observed in the study. For further details, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: AddressHate donates $75,000 to Baruch College's Antisemitism Studies Lab, helping catalyze nearly $500,000 in funding.
data.description: Baruch College in New York received a generous donation of $75,000 from AddressHate, a leading non-profit entity specializing in innovative methods to combat online hate. The contribution is designated to bolster The Antisemitism Studies Laboratory situated in Baruch's Sandra Kahn Wasserman Jewish Studies Center. AddressHate's pledge aims to enhance Baruch's capabilities in researching and addressing the growing concern of modern antisemitism, especially in the online sphere.

data.title: CIM Group buys 3 industrial buildings totaling 290,000 sf from Dynatect Manufacturing Inc. in a sale/leaseback deal.
data.description: In Los Angeles, CIM Group has purchased three industrial properties encompassing around 290,000 square feet from Dynatect Manufacturing Inc. in a sale and leaseback deal.
